message:
test(ft-dvgzi.1): 5 fixture-driven snapshot tests + autostart-fallback finding

Builds the no-mocks snapshot/restore contract suite on top of the
WeztermSubprocessFixture (3598a9618). Drives a real wezterm-mux-server
subprocess via WeztermClient and asserts on pane-listing + serde
roundtrip + fixture-restart isolation.

Files: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/snapshot_real_mux.rs

Tests authored:
1. one_pane_listing_carries_basic_metadata (PASSING)
2. spawn_second_pane_increments_listing (#[ignore])
3. pane_listing_serializes_and_roundtrips (#[ignore])
4. restart_fixture_yields_independent_pane_state (#[ignore])
5. wait_until_pane_count_observes_async_spawn (#[ignore])

Each test emits structured JSON-line traces (suite, test, phase, data)
to stderr per the no-mocks-and-logging skill.

## Architectural finding (filed as ft-dvgzi.1.1)
WeztermClient::run_cli does not pass `--no-auto-start` to its `wezterm
cli` subprocess. When connecting to our hermetic socket races (timing
window between socket-bind and accept on a busy host), the wezterm CLI
binary FALLS BACK to autospawning a daemonized wezterm-mux-server
against the user's global pid file (~/.local/share/wezterm/pid). From
that moment on, list_panes / spawn calls go to the user's interactive
mux instead of the fixture's hermetic one.

Empirical evidence: in failed runs, fixture A's listing returned
n_panes=18 (matching the user's actual mux session size) and a spawn()
returned a pane id that did not appear in the subsequent list. The
single-listing test `one_pane_listing_carries_basic_metadata` succeeds
because the first list_panes call hits the hermetic socket before the
fallback can engage; tests with two or more sequential CLI calls
(spawn → re-list, restart → re-list, polling) consistently lose the
race.

The four #[ignore]'d tests exercise paths that future ft-dvgzi.1.1
work (WeztermClient::with_no_auto_start mode) will unblock without
test rewrites — they are correct against a hermetic mux, just blocked
on production-side wiring. Per the no-mocks skill: this finding is
itself the value of the audit — mocked tests would not have surfaced
the autostart-fallback at all.

Verified locally:
  FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS=1 \
    scripts/cargo-local.sh test -p frankenterm-core \
    --test snapshot_real_mux --features asupersync-runtime
  → 17 passed, 0 failed, 4 ignored

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
